name: Ottoman Turkish (1500-1928)

ancestors.notes: Descent per Glottolog classification of Ottoman Turkish (otto1234).

notes: Skeleton. Ottoman Turkish, the Arabic-script form of Turkish (Turkic) used until 1928. Consonant letters are mapped to IPA, including the Perso-Turkish additions پ=[p], چ=[tʃ], ژ=[ʒ], گ=[ɡ] and sağır kef ڭ=[ŋ]; the Arabic emphatic and pharyngeal letters ث ص ض ط ظ ح ع fold into plain Turkish consonants. The script is an abjad for Turkish vowels: the eight-vowel harmony system is carried underspecified by و ی ا ه, so vowel quality is largely unrecoverable and a high PER floor is expected. Front/back readings of ك/ق and گ/غ, vowel length in Arabic/Persian loans, and stress are left unencoded.

sources.author: Hammarström, H. et al. (Glottolog 5.0)
sources.title: Glottolog: Ottoman Turkish (otto1234)
sources.publisher: Max Planck Institute for Evolutionary Anthropology
